intake.context: Parent ω-cycle date-percent-3n-macos-bypass-audit fixed cli/scripts/blowup_profile.hexa _profile_stub() at L155 to emit cascade-safe `_profile_now_ns()`. Carry-forward: previously emitted instrumented artifacts still carry the bug. Need: (a) verify template post-fix state, (b) enumerate stale artifacts, (c) regenerate or sed-fix each, (d) add regression test.

falsifier_pre_registration.name: template-current-state-cascade-safe
falsifier_pre_registration.evidence: Read cli/scripts/blowup_profile.hexa L143-182: _profile_stub() emits _profile_now_ns() with 3-tier cascade — (1) `date +%s%N` with `.contains("N") == false` guard, (2) `python3 -c 'import time;print(time.time_ns())'` fallback, (3) `date +%s` * 1e9 final fallback. All `try { to_int(...) } catch _e {}` blocks guard the parse. Comment marker '// macOS-correct (date-percent-3n-macos-bypass-audit 2026-04-27)' present at L156.

falsifier_pre_registration.name: stale-artifacts-enumerated
falsifier_pre_registration.evidence: Two-pronged search: (1) grep for harness sentinel 'BLOWUP PROFILE HARNESS' across nexus → only template itself matches; (2) grep for emitted function names '_profile_now_ns|_profile_emit|_profile_path' across nexus → only template + parent witness JSON match. Conclusion: ZERO persisted instrumented artifacts in tree. cmd_instrument writes to user-supplied out_path (no default location); blowup_phase_profile.hexa writes /tmp/blowup_profile_<pid>.ndjson (ephemeral). No on-disk stale artifact exists.

falsifier_pre_registration.name: each-artifact-regenerated-or-fixed
falsifier_pre_registration.evidence: F2 enumerated zero stale artifacts → vacuously satisfied. The /tmp ephemeral output files (if any from past sessions) will be regenerated on next run with the fixed template; they have no semantic persistence and cannot be reliably re-emitted (input drill state long gone — OBSTRUCTION_DOCUMENTED for ephemeral /tmp files: not regenerable, not persistent, future runs auto-fix.)

falsifier_pre_registration.name: regression-test-added-or-RCA-why-not
falsifier_pre_registration.evidence: Added `selftest` subcommand to cli/scripts/blowup_profile.hexa CLI dispatch. Calls _profile_stub() and asserts: (1) does NOT contain `date +%s%3N` (the historic bug token), (2) DOES contain `python3 -c 'import time;print(time.time_ns())'` (cascade fallback marker), (3) DOES contain `date +%s")` (final fallback). Run output: 3/3 PASS, exit 0. Future regressions of L155 will fail this selftest.

falsifier_pre_registration.name: grep-across-nexus-now-shows-zero-unguarded-date-percent-N-in-non-template-non-source
falsifier_pre_registration.evidence: Excluding source files (audited in parent cycle) and the templates themselves, grep -r 'date +%s%3N|date +%s%N' across nexus tree finds zero generated/emitted artifact files. The 41 hits are: template (1), parent witness (1), this witness (1), and 38 source files (under-source-audit, parent cycle scope). No emitted artifact carries the bug.

axes_2.name: template-correctness
axes_2.value: L143-182 cascade verified by direct read + selftest 3/3 PASS

axes_2.name: artifact-coverage
axes_2.value: 0 stale artifacts on disk (harness sentinel grep + emitted-function-name grep both empty); /tmp ephemeral output non-persistent

tier_1_findings.claim: _profile_stub() in cli/scripts/blowup_profile.hexa (L143-182) emits cascade-safe `_profile_now_ns()` with three explicit tiers: BSD `date +%s%N` (with `.contains("N") == false` guard) → python3 `time.time_ns()` → `date +%s` * 1e9. The 'macOS-correct' comment + audit topic-slug at L156 is the durable marker. Selftest exit 0 with 3 explicit PASS lines.

No persisted instrumented artifacts exist in the nexus tree. cmd_instrument writes to user-supplied out_path (no default writeback location); the only callers in this repo invoke it ad-hoc with a /tmp path or never (no scripted instrument-to-tracked-path pipeline exists). blowup_phase_profile.hexa explicitly writes /tmp/blowup_profile_<pid>.{ndjson,raw}. Conclusion: the carry-forward concern was prophylactic — the template fix has no replication-debt downstream.

New `selftest` subcommand (cli/scripts/blowup_profile.hexa, CLI dispatch block) makes the cascade requirement self-verifying. CI/manual run: `hexa run cli/scripts/blowup_profile.hexa selftest`. Three checks pin three properties: bug-token absent, python3 fallback present, date+%s fallback present. Future template edits that drop any tier will fail loudly.

edits.summary: Added `selftest` subcommand to CLI dispatch (between `analyze` and the help fallback). Calls _profile_stub() and asserts cascade properties. Updated help text to list new subcommand.

verifications.step: grep harness sentinel 'BLOWUP PROFILE HARNESS' across nexus

carry_forward: Consider linking selftest into a higher-level CI gate (e.g., pre-commit hook for cli/scripts/blowup_profile.hexa edits) so the cascade properties are auto-verified on each template change
Apply the same selftest pattern to other code-emitting templates in nexus (template-emits-shell pattern is generalizable)
Parent cycle's carry-forward 'raw .sh scripts (falsifier_health.sh, atlas_index.sh)' still open
obstructions_documented: Ephemeral /tmp/nexus_blowup_profile.ndjson and /tmp/blowup_profile_<pid>.ndjson trace files from prior runs cannot be 'regenerated' — they were captures of past drill executions whose seed/state is gone. They do not persist across reboots or new sessions. Future runs auto-use the fixed template. No semantic loss; no on-tree replication-debt.
